blocks.content: Lyria answered without hesitation.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Think of it simply. Evil is anything that rejects good.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Really? Then what is good?

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Anything that rejects evil.
Understanding Dante's silence, Lyria explained further.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: I get that question a lot. But it's because people try to analyze it in only one way. Logic is just one way humans understand the world. If you look more broadly, you'll accept things beyond logic.
Lyria crossed her index fingers in an X shape.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Good and evil define each other and prove their existence. Without good, how would you know what evil is? Conversely, the concept of good becomes clear because evil exists.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hmm, so the two opposing concepts support each other's existence.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Exactly. Mages call this the 'Law of Numbers.'

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: The Law of Numbers?

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Every number has meaning. At first, there was nothing, or maybe infinity. The number that represents this is 0. Then, as unique names like good, evil, Dante, and Lyria were given, things began to exist. That's 1. That's why a sealing formation must include the caster's name.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hmm, a unique name. Then what about 2?

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: The reason you couldn't define good and evil is that you saw them as separate 1s. But if you combine them into the concept of 2, that becomes the law.
Lyria held up three fingers and continued.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Lyria is 1, and the law is 2. Therefore, 3 is the number that finds a perfect self within the law. In the shrine, we call this the Trinity. Of course, reaching a perfect Trinity is difficult.
Lyria interlocked her fingers to form a triangle.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Everything in this world starts from the number 3. This is the Law of Numbers. It's the essence of the Akeanis Shrine and the first lesson given to exorcists.
Using the Law of Numbers, one can represent all the principles of the universe.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: It's similar to how Dante structures the world with 0s and 1s. And this is the insight of the mages.
Lyria continued.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: A sealing formation is different from a magic circle. Only those who have refined their minds through endless training can trap evil. That's why it's a spiritual power. Of course, this is also why it's not recognized academically.
Dante, having organized his thoughts, nodded.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: I understand. So what do I need to do?

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: I might need your help if the disaster materializes through necromantic summoning. Once it materializes, it will affect reality. Set up a magic circle and protect me if I get in danger. You don't have to save me, but if the spell is interrupted before the sealing, it will be fatal. How long do you need to prepare?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hmm, not long. I can start now.
Seeing Lyria's puzzled look, Dante chuckled.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: I don't like large magic circles. I usually use instant magic circles.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Lyria nodded in understanding.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Instant magic circles might be less powerful, but they offer flexibility in unexpected situations.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: In fact, exorcists also use makeshift sealing formations when necessary.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Alright. Let's begin.
As Lyria knelt and prepared to pray, Dante stepped back to brace for what was to come.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: O spirit lingering in the air, I bind your hands with the chains of holy power. Stay in place. Turn around. As the substance dedicates itself, a thousand masks will be removed.
Lyria's prayer continued endlessly.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: She strengthened the will of the substance to pressure the non-substance. Speaking the will to increase its intensity was similar to word magic.
With the mage's signature, the necromantic summoning began.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: In the name of Lyria, I command you to reveal yourself.
Dante expanded his Spirit Zone and prepared for battle.
For a minute, nothing happened.
But since Lyria remained in her praying position, he didn't relax.
Soon, he felt the ground tremble.
The vibrations weren't strong, but they covered a vast area. Snow on the Ice Queen's castle in the distance fell like dust.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Damn! Come out if you're coming out.
Dante surrounded the area with a defensive magic circle.
As time passed, the vibrations grew stronger. But still, there was no sign of the substance.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Ugh...
A groan escaped Lyria's lips.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: She was exerting all her strength, squeezing out every drop of her spirit to push something into reality.
Suddenly, the scenery flashed blue. Finally, a massive electric grid spread across the night sky.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Gasp!
Lyria's upper body jerked as she gasped for breath.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Was that the substance of the disaster? If so, should he fight it?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: But how could he fight something like that?

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: What is it? You need to tell me so I can act.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: I was mistaken. That's not an evil spirit. It's a pure mental entity.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: A mental entity? Explain it so I can understand.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: It has a neutral will, not biased towards either side. I can't seal it in this state. But it seems to be quickly absorbing the law of this place.
Dante observed the signals exchanging through the electromagnetic network.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Damn, what is this?
There wasn't a single repeated pattern.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: It was evidence that the entropy of the information the law was learning was extremely low.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: How could that be?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: ...It's not of this world. It came from somewhere else.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Then we must seal it now! If we leave it, it will quickly absorb the world's evil! I'll attempt the sealing formation!
As Lyria knelt again, a sound like nails on a chalkboard came from the sky.
The sharp noise found its frequency, creating a bizarre sound wave that no human could produce.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Who... am I?
Dante's face grew serious.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: The question 'Who am I?' meant it had developed self-awareness.
So what would happen next?
Before he could organize his thoughts, a simple yet blind shockwave, devoid of any individuality, struck the ground.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Damn it!
Dante set up a defensive magic circle around Lyria.
The mental entity's shockwave hit the ground with immense density, spreading out in a ring and generating a strong wind.
Swept away by the powerful wind, Dante was thrown back.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Dante!
A panicked Lyria ran to him.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: The mental entity disappeared into the night sky, but she couldn't even think of chasing it.
Tears welled up in Lyria's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: She had brought Dante into this. He had also kept his promise to protect her until the sealing was complete.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: It was her mistake in misidentifying the enemy's substance that put Dante in danger.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: If anything happened to him, it would all be her fault.
At that moment, Dante sat up, rubbing his head.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Ouch, my head. That scared me.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Dante, are you okay?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Whew, that's why instant magic circles are great.
Dante showed his palm. A coin-sized defensive magic circle glowed blue.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: In the moment of crisis, he had structured a defensive magic circle in the shortest time, splitting the shockwave.
Lyria finally understood the situation.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: But she wondered if it was possible to do that in a split second without any margin for error.
Exhausted, she collapsed into a sitting position.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Thank goodness. I'm sorry. Do you know how scared I was, thinking you were dead?

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: If I could die from this, I'd have died a hundred times already. But what was that? How can a mental entity produce a shockwave?

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: It was probably the law. It seemed like a defensive instinct that emerged with its self-awareness.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: The law? How can the law produce such monstrous destructive power? It was almost at the level of magic.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Mages who use the law strengthen the opposing concept to pressure the concept they want to attack. So if magic artificially twists phenomena, the law twists phenomena according to the natural order.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: It was probably a law that transcends human limits. If humans accept the law as 2, it accepts it as 1. It has always been the law. It must have existed as a mental entity for eons. Now that it has materialized, the strength of the law is different.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: So that's why the information entropy was so low. If it were of this world, it would have appeared by now. Anyway, the lower the entropy, the faster the changes will occur.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: Even without understanding information entropy, Lyria agreed that time was of the essence.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: That's the problem. We need to capture it as soon as possible. Now that it has materialized, it will surely transform into something that can destroy the world.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: But... it's still in the early stages, right? There's no guarantee it will turn into evil.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: High Priestess Poriter can't see all the world's disasters. But once she sees the future, it's never wrong. She said it would bring about the end. I'm certain that mental entity will become evil.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hmm, I see. Well... we wouldn't have known something was here if it weren't for Poriter. So, what do we do now?

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: There was something she needed to address before they proceeded with the exorcism.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: You're really strong. Honestly, I didn't expect much help from a student.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: What? Suddenly? You don't need to say that; just tell me the plan.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: No, I'm serious. I've been involved in many exorcisms with the Order. I know how powerful that shockwave was. Without you, we wouldn't have made it this far. On behalf of the Akeanis Shrine, which follows the path of good, I thank you.
Lyria bowed respectfully with her hands clasped.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Alright, enough of that. What do we do now? It's already materialized, so necromantic summoning won't work, right?

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: To the newly born mental entity, this place is like a cradle. It won't leave easily. We don't have much time, though.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hmm, what about going out and getting help? No one believed you earlier, but now they can see the disaster for themselves.

blocks.speaker: Lyria
blocks.content: It's too late. By the time we get back from the city, everything will be over. If the mental entity reaches the stage of intelligent reasoning, even I won't be able to hold it.
